analysisResults.summary: This prescription is for managing your Type 2 Diabetes and high blood pressure. The doctor has prescribed a combination of three medications to regulate your blood glucose levels, protect your stomach lining, and control your blood pressure. Consistent timing (especially before vs. after meals) is crucial for these medications.
recommendedActions: Monitor fasting and post-prandial blood sugar levels twice a week.
Adopt a low-sodium, low-glycemic diet. Restrict processed sugar and salt.
Incorporate 30 minutes of moderate physical activity (brisk walking) daily.
Stay well hydrated throughout the day.
analysisResults.followUp: Return for a follow-up consultation in 4 weeks with fresh Fasting Blood Sugar (FBS) and HbA1c reports.

analysisResults.summary: Your lipid (cholesterol) profile indicates elevated levels of total cholesterol and LDL ("bad") cholesterol, alongside borderline low HDL ("good") cholesterol. This combination increases cardiovascular risks and suggests a need for dietary modifications and regular screening.
recommendedActions: Reduce intake of saturated fats and trans fats (oils, butter, fried foods).
Increase intake of soluble fiber (oats, beans, lentils, fruits).
Consume omega-3 rich foods like walnuts, flaxseeds, or fish.
analysisResults.followUp: Discuss these results with your general physician to evaluate if statin therapy is required.

analysisResults.summary: Analysis of your lab report "labreportnew.pdf" indicates high total cholesterol and high LDL (bad cholesterol), alongside low levels of HDL (good cholesterol). This profile increases the risk of fatty build-ups in arteries, requiring lifestyle adjustments and physician review.
recommendedActions: Cut down on saturated fats (saturated cooking oils, ghee, butter, and red meat).
Increase consumption of soluble fiber like oats, beans, legumes, and green vegetables.
Engage in cardiovascular exercises (jogging, cycling, swimming) for 30 minutes 5 days a week.
Incorporate walnuts, almonds, and chia seeds (rich in healthy fats) into your diet.
analysisResults.followUp: Consult a primary care physician to discuss if cholesterol-lowering medication (statins) is needed. Repeat test in 8 weeks.

medications.name: Metformin
medications.description: Take Metformin 500mg twice daily after meals (usually after breakfast and after dinner).
medications.purpose: Helps lower blood sugar levels by improving how your body handles insulin.

medications.name: Telmisartan
medications.description: Take Telmisartan 40mg once daily in the morning, with or without food. Maintain a consistent time.
medications.purpose: Relaxes blood vessels to lower your blood pressure and protect your kidneys.

medications.name: Pantocid (Pantoprazole)
medications.description: Take Pantocid 40mg once daily in the morning, 30 minutes before your first meal (breakfast).
medications.purpose: Reduces stomach acid to prevent heartburn or gastritis potentially caused by other medicines.

testResults.description: Total cholesterol is elevated. High cholesterol can lead to plaque buildup in arteries.
LDL cholesterol is significantly high, which increases risks of heart disease.
HDL cholesterol is low. HDL helps clear bad cholesterol from your bloodstream, so higher is better.
Your triglycerides are currently within the healthy normal range.
Your total cholesterol is elevated. Levels above 200 increase risks of heart health concerns.
Your LDL (bad cholesterol) is high. It can form plaques in your blood vessels.
Your HDL (good cholesterol) is low. HDL helps clean up bad cholesterol, so a higher number is desired.
Your triglycerides (type of fat in blood) are currently within the healthy normal range.

warnings.title: Hypoglycemia Risk (Low Blood Sugar)
warnings.message: Since you are taking Metformin, watch out for symptoms of low blood sugar such as dizziness, sweating, shaking, or sudden hunger. Keep a sweet candy handy.

warnings.title: Strict Timing for Pantocid
warnings.message: Pantocid must be taken BEFORE breakfast (AC) to work effectively. Taking it after food reduces its effectiveness.

warnings.title: Elevated Cardiovascular Risk
warnings.message: Your high LDL (165 mg/dL) and low HDL (35 mg/dL) significantly raise cardiovascular risk factors. Consult a doctor for active lifestyle planning.

warnings.title: Elevated Cardiovascular Risk Profile
warnings.message: The combination of high LDL (168) and low HDL (34) is a risk factor for heart health. Regular checkups and cardiovascular monitoring are highly recommended.
